name: Coqui TTS (XTTS)
pricing_notes: Open source (MPL-2.0); XTTS model requires accepting Coqui Public Model License

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub for model download; XTTS license acceptance required

performance.notes: GPU recommended; XTTS: real-time factor ~1x on GPU; voice cloning ~6s reference audio

agent_readiness.notes: Advanced open-source TTS with voice cloning (XTTS). XTTS-v2: clone any voice from 6 seconds of audio, 17 languages. YourTTS: zero-shot voice cloning. Multiple TTS models (Tacotron2, GlowTTS, VITS, Bark). REST API server mode. Streaming inference. The leading open-source TTS for voice cloning use cases. Note: original Coqui.ai company shutdown (2024) but community maintains the repo.

gotchas: XTTS requires accepting Coqui Public Model License (not fully open for commercial)
GPU required for acceptable latency with voice cloning
Less actively maintained since company closure
best_when: Voice cloning from short audio samples (6+ seconds)
Multi-language TTS with consistent voice (17 languages XTTS-v2)
When ElevenLabs cost is prohibitive and voice cloning needed
Self-hosted voice cloning for privacy-sensitive applications
avoid_when: Highest quality voice synthesis (ElevenLabs still leads)
CPU-only environments (too slow for voice cloning)
summary: Coqui TTS is the leading open-source TTS library with XTTS-v2 voice cloning from 6 seconds of reference audio. 17 languages. Multiple TTS architectures (VITS, XTTS, Bark). Note: original company shut down (2024), now community-maintained. Best for self-hosted voice cloning without ElevenLabs costs. GPU required. XTTS model has non-commercial license restrictions.
